metadata.scheme_name: Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC, EBC and DNT Students for studying in India

raw_data.details: The "Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC, EBC and DNT Students for studying in India" aims at providing financial assistance to students from the Backward Classes (BC), Economically Weaker Sections (EWS), and Denotified, Nomadic, and Denotified, Nomadic Tribes (DNTs) to pursue higher education. This scholarship aims to alleviate the financial burden on students belonging to these marginalized communities, enabling them to continue their studies and enhance their skills. The scholarship covers tuition fees, maintenance allowances, and other academic expenses. The financial support is extended to students studying in both government and private institutions.
raw_data.benefits: Components of the ScholarshipMaintenance allowance for the complete duration of the course. Reimbursement of compulsory non-refundable fees. Study tour charges (if applicable). Thesis typing/printing charges (for research scholars). Book allowance for students pursuing correspondence courses. Additional allowances for students with disabilities. Financial Support BreakdownCategory of CoursesAcademic Allowance (Yearly in ₹)Tuition Fee (Yearly in ₹)Total (Yearly in ₹)Group 1: Degree and Post Graduate level professional courses10,00010,00020,000Group 2: Other Professional Courses leading to Degree, Diploma, Certificate8,0005,00013,000Group 3: Graduate and Post Graduate courses not covered under Group 1 & Group 26,0002,0008,000Group 4: All post-matriculation (Post Class X level) non-degree courses5,00005,000*Students pursuing C. A./I. C. W. A./C. S./I. C. F. A. shall be treated as day scholars for academic allowance purposes.*Students studying through correspondence/online courses shall not be eligible for academic allowance.*Tuition fees shall be paid to online/correspondence students.*The State Governments may, if they decide, top up the academic allowance from their own resources.*The scholarship amount will be disbursed annually to the bank account of the beneficiary.*The scholarship continues for the duration of the course, subject to satisfactory academic progress.
raw_data.eligibility: The applicant should be from Other Backward Classes (OBC) / Economically Backward Classes (EBC) or from Denotified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Tribes (DNT). The applicant should be studying at the post-matriculation or post-secondary stage of education. The applicant should be permanently settled in the State/UT where they are applying. The applicant should be studying in an institution recognized by the All India Survey on Higher Education (AISHE) or Unified District Information System for Education (UDISE). The applicant's institution should have National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC) or National Board of Accreditation (NBA) accreditation. The applicant should maintain Aadhaar-based attendance in the institution. The applicant must have a valid "Freeship Card" issued by the State/District Authority. Preference/Priority/Reservation/Relaxation:A maximum of 20 slots per annum are reserved for Commercial Pilot License courses, including helicopter and multi-engine training.
raw_data.exclusions: Scholarships are not applicable for studies outside India. Institutions without AISHE/UDISE codes are not eligible under the scheme.
raw_data.application_process:
OnlineRegistration
Step 1: Visit the [Official Website](https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in/) of the "Centralized Scholarship Portal" of the Department of Higher Education, Haryana.
Step 2: In the menu ribbon, click "Student > [Student Registration](https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in/StudentRegistration)".
Step 3: On the next page, provide your Parivar Pehchan Patra (Family ID) and click "Search".*Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP) is mandatory for availing of any Scholarship benefit. Students are advised to ensure their all particulars such as Name, DOB, Aadhar No. etc are fully updated in PPP before applying for any scholarship scheme.
Step 4: A List of the members of your family will be displayed. Select the checkbox against your name and click "Generate OTP". Enter the OTP received on your registered Mobile Number and click on "Verify OTP".
Step 5: After OTP verification, you will be taken to the registration page. In the registration form, all the mandatory fields get auto-filled. You only need to cross-check the information.
Step 6: Select your Department and College. Create a Password and click "Register". Application
Step 1: Visit the [Official Website](https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in/) of the "Centralized Scholarship Portal" of the Department of Higher Education, Haryana.
Step 2: In the right corner of the menu ribbon, click "[User Login](https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in/UserLogin)". Use the username and password created during your registration to log in.
Step 3: Navigate to the online form of the scheme you intend to apply for. In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. Carefully review all the information provided and the documents uploaded. Make any necessary corrections.
Step 4: Click "Apply" to submit your application. You'll receive a confirmation message.
raw_data.documents_required: Aadhaar CardCaste CertificateDomicile CertificateAdmission Letter from the Recognized InstitutionFee ReceiptFreeship Card, issued by State/District AuthorityBank Account Details, for Direct Benefit TransferPrevious Academic MarksheetsAccreditation Proof of Institution (For verification of NAAC/NBA accreditation)

EligibilityThe applicant should be from Other Backward Classes (OBC) / Economically Backward Classes (EBC) or from Denotified, Nomadic, and Semi-Nomadic Tribes (DNT). The applicant should be studying at the post-matriculation or post-secondary stage of education. The applicant should be permanently settled in the State/UT where they are applying. The applicant should be studying in an institution recognized by the All India Survey on Higher Education (AISHE) or Unified District Information System for Education (UDISE). The applicant's institution should have National Assessment and Accreditation Council (NAAC) or National Board of Accreditation (NBA) accreditation. The applicant should maintain Aadhaar-based attendance in the institution. The applicant must have a valid "Freeship Card" issued by the State/District Authority. ExclusionsScholarships are not applicable for studies outside India. Institutions without AISHE/UDISE codes are not eligible under the scheme. Application ProcessOnlineRegistrationStep 1: Visit the [Official Website](https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in/) of the "Centralized Scholarship Portal" of the Department of Higher Education, Haryana. Step 2: In the menu ribbon, click "Student > [Student Registration](https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in/StudentRegistration)". Step 3: On the next page, provide your Parivar Pehchan Patra (Family ID) and click "Search".*Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP) is mandatory for availing of any Scholarship benefit. Students are advised to ensure their all particulars such as Name, DOB, Aadhar No. etc are fully updated in PPP before applying for any scholarship scheme. Step 4: A List of the members of your family will be displayed. Select the checkbox against your name and click "Generate OTP". Enter the OTP received on your registered Mobile Number and click on "Verify OTP". Step 5: After OTP verification, you will be taken to the registration page. In the registration form, all the mandatory fields get auto-filled. You only need to cross-check the information. Step 6: Select your Department and College. Create a Password and click "Register". ApplicationStep 1: Visit the [Official Website](https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in/) of the "Centralized Scholarship Portal" of the Department of Higher Education, Haryana. Step 2: In the right corner of the menu ribbon, click "[User Login](https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in/UserLogin)". Use the username and password created during your registration to log in. Step 3: Navigate to the online form of the scheme you intend to apply for. In the form, fill in all the mandatory fields and upload all the mandatory documents in the specified format and size. Carefully review all the information provided and the documents uploaded. Make any necessary corrections. Step 4: Click "Apply" to submit your application. You'll receive a confirmation message. Documents RequiredAadhaar CardCaste CertificateDomicile CertificateAdmission Letter from the Recognized InstitutionFee ReceiptFreeship Card, issued by State/District AuthorityBank Account Details, for Direct Benefit TransferPrevious Academic MarksheetsAccreditation Proof of Institution (For verification of NAAC/NBA accreditation)Frequently Asked QuestionsWhat is the purpose of the Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme for OBC, EBC, and DNT students? The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to students from OBC, EBC, and DNT communities pursuing post-matric and post-secondary education by covering tuition fees, hostel fees, and other educational expenses. Which students are eligible for this scholarship? Students belonging to OBC, EBC, and DNT categories who are pursuing post-matric or post-secondary education in recognized institutions are eligible. What are the components covered under this scholarship? The scholarship includes academic allowance, tuition fee reimbursement, study tour charges, thesis typing/printing charges, book allowance, and additional allowances for students with disabilities. Are online or correspondence students eligible for the scholarship? Students pursuing online or correspondence courses are not eligible for academic allowances but can receive tuition fee reimbursement. What is the maximum number of slots available for Commercial Pilot License courses? A maximum of 20 slots per annum are reserved for students pursuing Commercial Pilot License courses. What is a "Freeship Card," and why is it required? A Freeship Card is issued by State/District Authorities to eligible students, ensuring their admission without upfront fee payment. What documents are required for the scholarship application? Applicants must provide caste certificate, income certificate, Aadhaar-linked bank account details, and proof of admission in a recognized institution. How can students apply for this scholarship? Eligible students must apply through their respective State/UT government portals as per the official notification. Who implements this scholarship scheme? The scheme is implemented by the respective State/Union Territory Governments in collaboration with the Government of India. Can State Governments provide additional financial support? Yes, State Governments may choose to top up the academic allowance from their own resources. Sources And References[Guideline](https://harchhatravratti.highereduhry.ac.in/Notices/PMS%20BC%20EWS%20DNT.pdf)OkWas this helpful?
raw_data.scheme_name: Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC, EBC and DNT Students for studying in India

faqs.question: What is the purpose of the Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme for OBC, EBC, and DNT students?
faqs.answer: The scheme aims to provide financial assistance to students from OBC, EBC, and DNT communities pursuing post-matric and post-secondary education by covering tuition fees, hostel fees, and other educational expenses.

faqs.question: Which students are eligible for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students belonging to OBC, EBC, and DNT categories who are pursuing post-matric or post-secondary education in recognized institutions are eligible.

faqs.question: What are the components covered under this scholarship?
faqs.answer: The scholarship includes academic allowance, tuition fee reimbursement, study tour charges, thesis typing/printing charges, book allowance, and additional allowances for students with disabilities.

faqs.question: Are online or correspondence students eligible for the scholarship?
faqs.answer: Students pursuing online or correspondence courses are not eligible for academic allowances but can receive tuition fee reimbursement.

faqs.question: What is the maximum number of slots available for Commercial Pilot License courses?
faqs.answer: A maximum of 20 slots per annum are reserved for students pursuing Commercial Pilot License courses.

faqs.question: What is a "Freeship Card," and why is it required?
faqs.answer: A Freeship Card is issued by State/District Authorities to eligible students, ensuring their admission without upfront fee payment.

faqs.question: What documents are required for the scholarship application?
faqs.answer: Applicants must provide caste certificate, income certificate, Aadhaar-linked bank account details, and proof of admission in a recognized institution.

faqs.question: How can students apply for this scholarship?
faqs.answer: Eligible students must apply through their respective State/UT government portals as per the official notification.

faqs.question: Who implements this scholarship scheme?
faqs.answer: The scheme is implemented by the respective State/Union Territory Governments in collaboration with the Government of India.

faqs.question: Can State Governments provide additional financial support?
faqs.answer: Yes, State Governments may choose to top up the academic allowance from their own resources.
